input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## `AppThemeBinding` in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension on any property:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#0078D4, Dark=#4CC2FF}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

Every `AppThemeBinding` takes a `Light` and `Dark` value. The framework watches `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` and swaps values automatically when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent** — use `SetAppTheme<T>()` extension method:

```csharp
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme<Color>(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,   // Light
    Colors.White);  // Dark

var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme<Color>(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"),  // Light
    Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E")); // Dark
```

---

**Tips:**

| Concern | Recommendation |
|---|---|
| **Reuse colors** | Define them in `App.xaml` as `<Color>` resources and reference with `{StaticResource}` inside the `AppThemeBinding` |
| **Scaled apps** | Use `ResourceDictionary` with separate light/dark dictionaries merged via `AppThemeBinding` |
| **Force a theme** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to override the system setting |
| **Detect at runtime** | Read `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (returns `AppTheme.Light`, `Dark`, or `Unspecified`) |

No platform-specific code or custom renderers required — `AppThemeBinding` handles iOS, Android, Windows, and macOS uniformly.
